004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.

006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.objecttools;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
020
021import java.util.*;
022import java.util.regex.*;
023
024import org.apache.juneau.commons.lang.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
027
028/**
029 * String matcher factory for the {@link ObjectSearcher} class.
030 *
031 * <p>
032 *    The class provides searching based on the following patterns:
033 * </p>
034 * <ul>
035 *    <li><js>"property=foo"</js> - Simple full word match
036 *    <li><js>"property=fo*"</js>, <js>"property=?ar"</js> - Meta-character matching
037 *    <li><js>"property=foo bar"</js>(implicit), <js>"property=^foo ^bar"</js>(explicit) - Multiple OR'ed patterns
038 *    <li><js>"property=+fo* +*ar"</js> - Multiple AND'ed patterns
039 *    <li><js>"property=fo* -bar"</js> - Negative patterns
040 *    <li><js>"property='foo bar'"</js> - Patterns with whitespace
041 *    <li><js>"property=foo\\'bar"</js> - Patterns with single-quotes
042 *    <li><js>"property=/foo\\s+bar"</js> - Regular expression match
043 * </ul>
044 *
045 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
046 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/ObjectTools">Object Tools</a>
047 * </ul>
048 */
049@SuppressWarnings({
050   "java:S6541", // Stateless utility, singleton for convenience
051   "java:S6542"  // Singleton required for stateless MatcherFactory; shared instance in ObjectSearcher
052})
053public class StringMatcherFactory extends MatcherFactory {
054
055   /**
056    * A construct representing a single search pattern.
057    */
058   private static class StringMatcher extends AbstractMatcher {
059      private static final AsciiSet META_CHARS = AsciiSet.of("*?'\"");
060      private static final AsciiSet SQ_CHAR = AsciiSet.of("'");
061      private static final AsciiSet DQ_CHAR = AsciiSet.of("\"");
062      private static final AsciiSet REGEX_CHARS = AsciiSet.of("+\\[]{}()^$.");
063      private String pattern;
064
065      Pattern[] orPatterns;
066      Pattern[] andPatterns;
067      Pattern[] notPatterns;
068
069      @SuppressWarnings({
070         "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for string pattern matching
071      })
072      public StringMatcher(String searchPattern) {
073
074         this.pattern = searchPattern.trim();
075         var ors = new LinkedList<Pattern>();
076         var ands = new LinkedList<Pattern>();
077         var nots = new LinkedList<Pattern>();
078
079         for (var s : StringUtils.splitQuoted(pattern, true)) {
080            char c0 = s.charAt(0);
081         char c9 = s.charAt(s.length() - 1);
082
083            if (c0 == '/' && c9 == '/' && s.length() > 1) {
084               ands.add(Pattern.compile(strip(s)));
085            } else {
086               char prefix = '^';
087               boolean ignoreCase = false;
088               if (s.length() > 1 && (c0 == '^' || c0 == '+' || c0 == '-')) {
089                  prefix = c0;
090                  s = s.substring(1);
091                  c0 = s.charAt(0);
092               }
093
094               if (c0 == '\'') {
095                  s = unescapeChars(strip(s), SQ_CHAR);
096                  ignoreCase = true;
097               } else if (c0 == '"') {
098                  s = unescapeChars(strip(s), DQ_CHAR);
099               }
100
101               if (REGEX_CHARS.contains(s) || META_CHARS.contains(s)) {
102                  var sb = new StringBuilder();
103                  boolean isInEscape = false;
104                  for (var i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
105                     var c = s.charAt(i);
106                     if (isInEscape) {
107                        if (c == '?' || c == '*' || c == '\\')
108                           sb.append('\\').append(c);
109                        else
110                           sb.append(c);
111                        isInEscape = false;
112                     } else {
113                        if (c == '\\')
114                           isInEscape = true;
115                        else if (c == '?')
116                           sb.append(".?");
117                        else if (c == '*')
118                           sb.append(".*");
119                        else if (REGEX_CHARS.contains(c))
120                           sb.append("\\").append(c);
121                        else
122                           sb.append(c);
123                     }
124                  }
125                  s = sb.toString();
126               }
127
128               int flags = Pattern.DOTALL;
129               if (ignoreCase)
130                  flags |= Pattern.CASE_INSENSITIVE;
131
132               var p = Pattern.compile(s, flags);
133
134               if (prefix == '-')
135                  nots.add(p);
136               else if (prefix == '+')
137                  ands.add(p);
138               else
139                  ors.add(p);
140            }
141         }
142         orPatterns = ors.toArray(new Pattern[ors.size()]);
143         andPatterns = ands.toArray(new Pattern[ands.size()]);
144         notPatterns = nots.toArray(new Pattern[nots.size()]);
145      }
146
147      @Override
148      public boolean matches(ClassMeta<?> cm, Object o) {
149         var s = (String)o;
150         for (var andPattern : andPatterns)
151            if (! andPattern.matcher(s).matches())
152               return false;
153         for (var notPattern : notPatterns)
154            if (notPattern.matcher(s).matches())
155               return false;
156         for (var orPattern : orPatterns)
157            if (orPattern.matcher(s).matches())
158               return true;
159         return orPatterns.length == 0;
160      }
161
162      @Override
163      public String toString() {
164         return pattern;
165      }
166   }
167
168   /**
169    * Default reusable matcher.
170    */
171   @SuppressWarnings({
172      "java:S6541", // Stateless factory, singleton for convenience
173      "java:S6542"  // Singleton required for stateless MatcherFactory; shared instance in ObjectSearcher
174   })
175   public static final StringMatcherFactory DEFAULT = new StringMatcherFactory();
176
177   /**
178    * Constructor.
179    */
180   @SuppressWarnings({
181      "java:S6542"  // Protected constructor part of intentional singleton; subclassing allowed
182   })
183   protected StringMatcherFactory() {}
184
185   @Override
186   public boolean canMatch(ClassMeta<?> cm) {
187      return true;
188   }
189
190   @Override
191   public AbstractMatcher create(String pattern) {
192      return new StringMatcher(pattern);
193   }
194}
